title: Business, research and administrative professionals n.e.c.
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate, usually through subordinate supervisory personnel, activities concerned with the construction and maintenance of structures, facilities, and systems. Participate in the conceptual development of a construction project and oversee its organization, scheduling, budgeting, and implementation. Includes managers in specialized construction fields, such as carpentry or plumbing. Plan, direct, or coordinate entertainment and recreational activities and operations of a recreational facility, including cruise ships and parks. All personal service managers not listed separately. (Based on 25 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Develop or implement quality control and environmental protection programs for construction or administrative projects.
Confer with supervisory personnel, owners, contractors, or design professionals to discuss and resolve construction problems.
Apply green building strategies to reduce energy costs and minimize environmental impacts.
Prepare and submit budget estimates and progress reports for construction projects.
Inspect or review projects to monitor compliance with building codes and environmental regulations.
Plan, organize, or direct activities concerning construction, maintenance of structures, and operations of facilities.
Implement training programs on environmentally responsible and safety building topics to update employee skills.
Evaluate construction methods and determine cost-effectiveness using computer models.
Prepare contracts or negotiate revisions with stakeholders like architects, consultants, and subcontractors.
Monitor and document logistical activities, ensuring compliance with policies and procedures.
Develop and implement records management programs for efficient filing and retrieval of documents.
Collect and analyze logistics data to improve operational efficiency and propose logistical solutions.
Become familiar with customers' needs and actions to ensure those needs are met efficiently.
Manage subcontractor activities by reviewing proposals and serving as liaisons between parties involved in projects.
Stay informed of logistics advances to apply appropriate technologies improving processes.
Provide ongoing analyses in areas such as procurement, manufacturing, and inventory management to improve service delivery.
Provide transcripts of proceedings upon request of judges, lawyers, or the public and verify accuracy by checking copies against original records.
Inspect food products and processing procedures to determine whether products are safe to eat and take emergency actions if product safety is compromised.
Conduct or oversee contingency plan integration and operation and develop emergency management plans for recovery decision making and communications.
Monitor compliance to operational, safety, or inventory control procedures, including physical security standards and advise retail managers on compliance with applicable codes, laws, or regulations.
Create business continuity and disaster recovery budgets and prepare reports summarizing operational results or financial performance.
Analyze corporate intelligence data to identify trends or patterns indicating threats to security of people, assets, or information.
Develop sustainability project goals, objectives, initiatives, or strategies in collaboration with other sustainability professionals.
Inspect fire, intruder detection, or other security systems and train personnel in security procedures or use of security equipment.
Review existing disaster recovery, crisis management, or business continuity plans, and test documented strategies and plans.
Manage warranty repair or replacement services and order parts, tools, or equipment needed to maintain operations.
Develop relationships with state or central government environmental regulatory agencies to analyze potential impacts of proposed policies.
Evaluate regulatory aspects related to green issues, such as toxic substances, carbon footprinting, and policy implementation.
Monitor regulatory affairs activities and trends to ensure alignment with corporate sustainability or green initiatives.
Organize and oversee fitness or wellness programs, including presentations and training in first aid or CPR.
Track cost-containment strategies and evaluate the effectiveness of wellness programs or services.
Assign tasks and work hours to staff while scheduling guest appointments and managing client databases.
Prepare or implement budgets for recreational activities or wellness programs, ensuring financial viability and operational plans.
Coordinate on-site activities for environmental cleanup projects, ensuring compliance with regulations and standards.
Design or implement measures for environmental restoration, contamination remediation, or structural demolition projects.
Conduct periodic reviews or audits to ensure compliance procedures are followed and develop strategies for compliance improvements.
Investigate or interview individuals suspected of compliance violations and provide recommendations for improvements in procedures.
Maintain documentation of compliance activities and regulatory submissions to ensure timely and accurate reporting.
Supervise fitness or wellness specialists and provide training in the use or sale of wellness programs or products.
Review and evaluate designs for compliance and conduct risk assessments related to environmental or operational risks.
Communicate regulatory information and ensure it is correctly interpreted by multiple departments within the organization.
Develop or implement marketing strategies for wellness programs or environmental services to promote participation or compliance.
Coordinate facility schedules to maximize usage and efficiency of recreational or wellness facilities.
Update schedules, estimates, forecasts, or budgets for wind projects and manage wind project costs to stay within budget limits.
